# Assignment Profile: CMJ 103 Speech 4 — Professional Context Speech

**Generated by:** assignment-analysis skill
**Student:** Emily Tran
**Course:** CMJ 103 Fundamentals of Public Communication, Fall 2026
**Professor:** Dr. Marcus Chen

---

## Assignment Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Title | Speech 4: Professional Context Speech |
| Weight | 20% of course grade |
| Format | Individual, in-person delivery to class |
| Duration | 5-7 minutes |
| Due Date | Week 14 |
| Deliverables | In-class speech delivery + visual aids |
| Gen-Ed | Written and Oral Communication (WOC) |

## Learning Outcomes

| Outcome | Bloom's Level | Action Verb |
|---|---|---|
| LO-1: Construct and deliver an organized, evidence-based oral presentation | Apply | Construct, Deliver |
| LO-2: Analyze audience and adapt communication strategies accordingly | Analyze | Analyze, Adapt |
| LO-4: Demonstrate effective use of visual aids and supporting materials | Apply | Demonstrate |

Gen-Ed Mapping: WOC (Written and Oral Communication)

Note: Speech 4 is NOT the gen-ed signature assignment (that is Speech 2). It does not have the UMaine WOC standardized rubric requirement.

## Rubric Criteria

| Criterion | Points | Performance Indicators |
|---|---|---|
| Delivery | 25 | Eye contact, vocal variety, pacing, physical presence, confidence. High: natural and engaging. Low: reads from notes, monotone, rushed. |
| Organization | 25 | Clear introduction, logical body structure, smooth transitions, strong conclusion. High: easy to follow, transitions signal structure. Low: disorganized, abrupt shifts, no clear conclusion. |
| Visual aids and supporting materials | 15 | Aids enhance the message, are professional quality, integrated smoothly. High: aids clarify complex points, speaker references them naturally. Low: cluttered slides, aids not referenced, technical difficulties. |
| Audience adaptation | 20 | Content pitched to audience's knowledge level, jargon explained, engagement techniques used. High: non-specialist audience understands the topic. Low: assumes knowledge audience lacks, no adjustment. |
| Evidence use | 15 | Claims supported with data, examples, or citations. High: diverse, credible sources integrated naturally. Low: unsupported claims, sources not cited, single source. |

## Format Requirements

- Individual presentation (not group)
- 5-7 minutes in length (timed, points deducted for under/over)
- In-person delivery to class section (approximately 30 students)
- Visual aids required (typically slides, but format open)
- Topic: present on a subject in the student's field to a non-specialist audience
- No written component submitted (speech delivery only)

## Course Context

- Speech 4 is the final individual speech in a progression: Informative (Speech 1) → Persuasive (Speech 2) → Group (Speech 3) → Professional Context (Speech 4)
- Builds on skills from all prior speeches
- Follows Week 13 content on "Communication in professional contexts"
- Final assessment before the reflective portfolio (Week 15)
- Speech 2 (Persuasive) is the gen-ed signature assignment, not Speech 4

## Augmentation Readiness

| Element | Fixed or Flexible |
|---|---|
| Duration (5-7 min) | FIXED — timed, points deducted outside range |
| Individual delivery | FIXED — must be individual |
| In-person to class | FIXED — live delivery required |
| Visual aids | FLEXIBLE — format open (slides, demo, poster, props) |
| Topic | FLEXIBLE — "a topic in your field" allows broad interpretation |
| Rubric criteria | FIXED — all 5 criteria assessed as-is |
| Submission format | FIXED — in-class delivery, no written submission |

**Summary:** The topic and visual aid format are flexible. Everything else — duration, delivery mode, rubric criteria — is fixed. Augmentation opportunities should focus on topic choice and visual aid format, not on modifying the assessment structure.
